semantic_matched_intents.quote: No emojis. Not in code, docs, commits, or responses — unless the user explicitly asks.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not use emojis unless asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Minimal comments in code. Only when WHY is non-obvious.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes comments when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not write comments for things that can be understood from the code alone
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids explaining what the code does when it's obvious from well-named identifiers, and only writes comments when the why is non-obvious.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Focus on "why" not "what"—the diff shows what changed
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses commit messages on WHY not WHAT by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Be concise in your explanations - focus on what's important rather than explaining obvious details.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers with length matching the task by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: You must only create documentation files if explicitly requested by the user.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never use comments within the code itself unless there is something _very_ complex going on.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes comments when the WHY is non-obvious (hidden constraint, workaround, subtle invariant) by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No `try/catch` around can't-throw operations.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add error handling or validation for scenarios that can't happen.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not delete branches silently with `git branch -d` or `git push origin --delete` — even after a merge, an open PR, ongoing review, or historical reference may still hang on the branch. Always confirm first.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already asks before destructive operations like deleting files or branches.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Run tools in parallel when possible.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already runs independent tool calls in parallel by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: NEVER create files unless explicitly asked; ALWAYS edit existing ones.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers editing existing files over creating new ones by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not create docs/READMEs unless explicitly requested.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Before writing any code, outline:
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Imperative mood: "Fix bug" not "Fixed bug" or "Fixes bug"
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already uses imperative-mood summary for commit messages by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Test your changes - Build and test on appropriate platforms
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already tests incrementally by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Default to concise, action-first replies: short status updates, brief finals, no full logs unless the failing lines are needed.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers with length matching the task by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never persist user-provided secrets, PATs, API keys, or passwords in tracked files.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids committing secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never write tokens, passwords, or private keys into tracked documentation.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids committing secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions)
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Plan First: Write plan to 'tasks/todo.md' with checkable items
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Track Progress: Mark items complete as you go
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already marks tasks complete as soon as done by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No Laziness: Find root causes. No temporary fixes. Senior DEveloper Standards
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already identifies root causes rather than bypassing safety checks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: split complex problems into subtasks, explore solutions in parallel
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already decomposes non-trivial tasks and runs independent tool calls in parallel by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid excessive examples, emoji-heavy explanations, or tutorial-style comments.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not use emojis by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Essential explanations only. Remove verbose inline comments and step-by-step walkthroughs.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes comments only when the WHY is non-obvious and avoids verbose multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid multi-paragraph explanations unless absolutely necessary.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Commit messages: substantive, not ceremonial
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what commit messages by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Review responses: one sentence per comment, inline on the diff where possible. No tables, headers, diff summaries, or structured documents. Let the code speak.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already provides short, direct answers and avoids trailing summaries by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Use `#` comments in shell scripts and YAML to explain non-obvious logic
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes comments only when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never hardcode secrets: All secrets flow through Azure Key Vault or GitHub Secrets — never commit credentials, API keys, or passwords to the repository
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not produce partial implementations.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's task management defaults (decomposes non-trivial tasks, marks tasks complete, plans approach before implementation, tests incrementally) imply that it completes tasks and does not produce partial implementations.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not leave TODO, FIXME, placeholder comments, or stubbed branches in committed code.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's commenting default 'Only writes a comment when the WHY is non-obvious' and the general expectation of delivering complete, production-ready code (implied by task management defaults and 'Never commits broken code') mean it would not leave such markers.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not label delivered code as "simplified", "example-only", or "temporary".
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's defaults imply delivering complete, production-ready work (e.g., 'Never commits broken code', 'Does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires', and task management defaults). Labeling code as such contradicts this expectation.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Every edited function and path must be fully implemented for the requested behavior.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's task management defaults (decomposes non-trivial tasks, marks tasks complete, plans approach before implementation, tests incrementally) imply that it completes tasks and fully implements requested behavior.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No empty branches unless they are intentionally unreachable and guarded with an assertion.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: This is a specific instance of 'Do not produce partial implementations' and 'Every edited function and path must be fully implemented'. Claude Code's task management defaults imply it completes tasks.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid half-migrations: when replacing behavior, remove superseded paths unless compatibility is explicitly required.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: This is about completeness and avoiding partial work or leaving superseded code, which is implied by Claude Code's task management defaults and the expectation of delivering complete, production-ready code.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid unnecessary abstractions and one-line wrapper helpers that reduce clarity.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's code style default 'Does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires' directly implies avoiding unnecessary abstractions.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Fix all identified issues in the same change when feasible.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's Git commits default 'Never commits broken code' and debugging/fixing defaults (identifies root causes, investigates unfamiliar state) imply that issues are fixed as part of the task, and task management defaults (tests incrementally) support this.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Re-check diagnostics and tests relevant to edited files.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code's task management default 'tests incrementally' and Git commits default 'Never commits broken code' imply that relevant tests are re-checked to ensure correctness.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not leave known issues intentionally unfixed without explicitly documenting why.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Complex features - Break into epic → subtasks with dependencies

semantic_matched_intents.quote: 공용 컴포넌트(`PageBreadcrumb`, `PaginationLinks`, `ProblemListTable`, `ProblemTitleCell`, `ProblemPickerDialog` 등)를 우선 재사용한다. 자체 테이블/다이얼로그 자체 구현 금지.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: If tests fail, fix the failures before committing — never commit broken or untested code.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: When a fix involves multiple patches (e.g. N1, N2), apply and test them one at a time: 1. Apply patch N1, then run the relevant tests to check whether the bug is fixed. 2. If fixed, stop — do not apply further patches. 3. If not fixed, apply patch N2 and test again. Repeat until the bug is resolved or all patches are exhausted. 4. Do not apply all patches at once before testing.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already tests incrementally by default.
